tools.name: Catch me up
tools.desc: What happened while I was away?
tools.prompt: Catch me up. Check my recent emails, calendar, and any notifications from my connected services. Summarize what needs my attention right now — prioritize by urgency. Be brief.

tools.name: Handle this email
tools.desc: I'll paste it, you draft the reply
tools.prompt: I'm going to paste an email I received. Read it, tell me what they want in one sentence, then draft a reply. Keep it professional and concise. Ask me for the email.

tools.name: Read this for me
tools.prompt: I have a document I need you to read. Give me: what it says in plain English, what I need to do about it, and anything I should be concerned about. Ask me to upload it.

tools.name: Reply to a customer
tools.desc: Turn a complaint into a happy customer
tools.prompt: I got a message from a customer that I need help responding to. I'll paste it. Write a reply that solves their problem and keeps them happy. Ask me for the message.

tools.name: Clean up my notes
tools.desc: Turn a mess into a clear action plan
tools.prompt: I have messy notes from a meeting or call. I'll paste them. Turn them into: a 3-sentence summary, a list of who needs to do what, and any deadlines. Ask me for the notes.

tools.name: Help me think through this
tools.desc: Talk through a decision or idea
tools.prompt: I need to think through something — could be a business decision, a strategy, a hire, a pricing change, whatever. Ask me what's on my mind and help me think it through. Give me honest pros and cons, not just what I want to hear.

tools.name: Look this up for me
tools.desc: Get the facts on anything, fast
tools.prompt: I need you to research something for me. Ask me what I want to know about. Then give me the key facts, organized clearly, with sources where possible. No fluff.

tools.name: Write something for me
tools.desc: Proposals, pitches, or any business doc
tools.prompt: I need you to write something — could be a proposal, a pitch, a scope of work, or any business document. Ask me who it's for and what I need, then write it so I can send it as-is.
